franchise.name: DC Franchise Database
franchise.description: Total Drama franchise statistics and season data

seasons.title: Total Drama 2
seasons.subtitle: The sequel that raised the stakes

seasons.title: Total Drama Revenge of the Island
seasons.subtitle: The season where loyalty was tested—and broken

winner.name: Jacques
winner.keyStats: 7–1–0 jury win; 4 individual immunities; 4 total challenge wins; only 2 votes against.
winner.strategy: Kept target low while steering vote flows, exploiting pair cover and timely immunity wins to consolidate control.
winner.legacy: A crowned villain: precise, low-agro control culminating in a blowout FTC. Benchmarks a ‘low-target, high-control’ masterclass for this format.

seasons.title: Total Drama Fans VS Favorites
seasons.subtitle: Same game. Different side of the screen

winner.name: MacArthur
winner.keyStats: 4 total challenge wins, 1 immunity, 1 idol found and timed perfectly, broad cross-tribe relationships; only 5 votes against all season.
winner.strategy: Operate as the merge switchboard and shield-enforcer: hold an idol, build unbreakables with Carrie and Cody, maintain bridges to Jacques and Sky, disagree publicly when needed to preserve agency, and close with perfect F5 advantage management.
winner.legacy: One of the most respected power-social winners of the series: balanced menace and loyalty, rode chaos without being consumed by it, and delivered a decisive FTC performance.

seasons.title: Total Drama Heroes VS Villains VS Civilians
seasons.subtitle: Good VS Evil VS Civilians

winner.name: Alejandro
winner.keyStats: Co-winner with 4 jury votes; 4 individual immunities; 7 total challenge wins; only 2 votes against.
winner.strategy: Built cross-tribe power with Pete and later a tight bond with Cameron, steering critical blindsides while avoiding exposure. When shields fell, he won out in challenges, cut Sanders at Final 3, and forced a legacy Final 2 that split the jury.
winner.legacy: Becomes the franchise’s first two-time champion (shared). Cemented as the definitive 'Villain King' who can both orchestrate and out-muscle in endgame.

winner.name: Cameron
winner.keyStats: Co-winner with 4 jury votes; 4 individual immunities; 7 total challenge wins; only 2 votes against.
winner.strategy: Built cross-tribe power with Pete and later a tight bond with Cameron, steering critical blindsides while avoiding exposure. When shields fell, he won out in challenges, cut Sanders at Final 3, and forced a legacy Final 2 that split the jury.
winner.legacy: Becomes the franchise’s first two-time champion (shared). Cemented as the definitive 'Villain King' who can both orchestrate and out-muscle in endgame.
